possible_causes: Ignition arc but no flame formation
Presence of flame but insufficient ionization (<1μΑ)

Check that the gas valve is fully opened
Purge the gas supply to remove air
Check the operation and setting of the gas valve unit
Check that the air inlet and flue gas discharge flues are not blocked
Check the wiring on the gas valve
Check the wiring on the ionization/ignition electrode

The burner remains very hot (CO2 too high)

Check the gas/air unit and replace if necessary

safety_warnings.text: This boiler can be used by children aged 8 years and above and by persons with reduced physical, sensory or mental capabilities or lack of experience and knowledge when they have been given supervision or instruction concerning the safe use of the device and understand the resulting risks. Children must not be allowed to play with the appliance. Cleaning and user maintenance must not be carried out by children without supervision.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger This boiler can be used by children aged 8 years and above and by persons with reduced physical, sensory or mental capabilities or lack of experience and knowledge when they have been given supervision or instruction concerning the safe use of the device and understand the resulting risks. Children must not be allowed to play with the appliance. Cleaning and user maintenance must not be carried out by children without supervision.

safety_warnings.text: If you smell gas: 1. Do not use a naked flame, do not smoke, do not operate electrical contacts or switches (doorbell, light, motor, lift, etc.). 2. Shut off gas supply. 3. Open the windows. 4. Trace possible leaks and seal them immediately. 5. If the gas leak is before the gas meter, contact the supplier 6. Telephone the National Gas Emergency Service on:- 0800 111 999.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger If you smell gas: 1. Do not use a naked flame, do not smoke, do not operate elec- trical contacts or switches (doorbell, light, motor, lift, etc.). 2. Shut off gas supply. 3. Open the windows. 4. Trace possible leaks and seal them immediately. 5. If the gas leak is before the gas meter, contact the supplier 6. Telephone the National Gas Emergency Service on:- 0800 111 999.

safety_warnings.text: If you smell flue gases: 1. Switch off the boiler. 2. Open the windows. 3. Trace possible leaks and seal them immediately.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger If you smell flue gases: 1. Switch off the boiler. 2. Open the windows. 3. Trace possible leaks and seal them immediately.

safety_warnings.text: Do not touch the flue gas pipes. Depending on the boiler settings, the temperature of the flue gas pipes may exceed 60°C.

source_refs.source_quote: Warning Do not touch the flue gas pipes. Depending on the boiler settings, the temperature of the flue gas pipes may exceed 60°C.

safety_warnings.text: Do not touch the radiators for long periods. Depending on the boiler settings, the temperature of the radiators may exceed 60°C.

source_refs.source_quote: Warning Do not touch the radiators for long periods. Depending on the boil- er settings, the temperature of the radiators may exceed 60°C.

safety_warnings.text: Take precautions with the domestic hot water. Depending on the boiler settings, the domestic hot water temperature may exceed 65°C.

source_refs.source_quote: Warning Take precautions with the domestic hot water. Depending on the boiler settings, the domestic hot water temperature may exceed 65°C.

safety_warnings.text: Before any work, switch off the mains supply to the boiler.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger Before any work, switch off the mains supply to the boiler.

safety_warnings.text: After maintenance or repair work, check the entire heating installation to ensure that there are no leaks.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution After maintenance or repair work, check the entire heating installa- tion to ensure that there are no leaks.

safety_warnings.text: Installation and maintenance of the boiler must be carried out by a qualified installer in accordance with local and national regulations.

source_refs.source_quote: Warning Installation and maintenance of the boiler must be carried out by a qualified installer in accordance with local and national regula- tions.

safety_warnings.text: If the mains lead is damaged, it must be replaced by the original manufacturer, the manufacturer's dealer or another suitably skilled person to prevent hazardous situations from arising.

source_refs.source_quote: Warning If the mains lead is damaged, it must be replaced by the original manufacturer, the manufacturer's dealer or another suitably skilled person to prevent hazardous situations from arising.

safety_warnings.text: Always disconnect the mains supply and close the main gas tap when working on the boiler.

source_refs.source_quote: Warning Always disconnect the mains supply and close the main gas tap when working on the boiler.

safety_warnings.text: Make sure the boiler can be reached at all times. The boiler must be installed in a frost-free area. In the case of a fixed connection to the power cord, you must always install a main bipolar switch with an opening gap of at least 3 mm (EN 60335-1). Drain the boiler and central heating system if you are not going to use your home for a long time and there is a chance of frost. The frost protection does not work if the boiler is out of operation. The boiler protection only protects the boiler, not the system. Check the water pressure in the system regularly. If the water pressure is lower than 0.8 bar, the system must be topped up (recommended water pressure between 1 and 2 bar).

source_refs.source_quote: Caution • Make sure the boiler can be reached at all times. • The boiler must be installed in a frost-free area. • In the case of a fixed connection to the power cord, you must al- ways install a main bipolar switch with an opening gap of at least 3 mm (EN 60335-1). • Drain the boiler and central heating system if you are not going to use your home for a long time and there is a chance of frost. • The frost protection does not work if the boiler is out of opera- tion. • The boiler protection only protects the boiler, not the system. • Check the water pressure in the system regularly. If the water pressure is lower than 0.8 bar, the system must be topped up (recommended water pressure between 1 and 2 bar).

safety_warnings.text: Risk of dangerous situations that may result in serious personal injury.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger Risk of dangerous situations that may result in serious personal injury.

safety_warnings.text: Risk of electric shock.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger of electric shock Risk of electric shock.

safety_warnings.text: Risk of dangerous situations that may result in minor personal injury.

source_refs.source_quote: Warning Risk of dangerous situations that may result in minor personal in- jury.

safety_warnings.text: Risk of material damage.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution Risk of material damage.

safety_warnings.text: Installation, repair and maintenance must only be carried out by a competent person. This document is intended for use by competent persons.

source_refs.source_quote: Warning Installation, repair and maintenance must only be carried out by a competent person. This document is intended for use by compe- tent persons.

safety_warnings.text: Some residual water may escape when removing the sealing caps. Take precautions to avoid damage to components!

source_refs.source_quote: Warning Some residual water may escape when removing the sealing caps. Take precautions to avoid damage to components!

safety_warnings.text: If the boiler is installed in a compartment do not use it for storage purposes. Do not obstruct any purpose provided ventilation openings.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger If the boiler is installed in a compartment do not use it for storage purposes. Do not obstruct any purpose provided ventilation open- ings.

safety_warnings.text: Check that the total nominal consumption of the accessories connected to the appliance is less than 1 amp. If it is higher, a relay must be installed between the accessories and the electronic board.

source_refs.source_quote: Warning Check that the total nominal consumption of the accessories con- nected to the appliance is less than 1 amp. If it is higher, a relay must be installed between the accessories and the electronic board.

safety_warnings.text: Only the initial de-aeration process during commissioning is performed automatically. Any subsequent de-aeration carried out, for example after a system drain down, must be done manually.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution Only the initial de-aeration process during commissioning is per- formed automatically. Any subsequent de-aeration carried out, for example after a system drain down, must be done manually.

safety_warnings.text: If the power is lost to the boiler during a manual de-aeration the process does NOT restart automatically! Re-establish the power and manually restart the de-aeration.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution If the power is lost to the boiler during a manual de-aeration the process does NOT restart automatically! Re-establish the power and manually restart the de-aeration.

safety_warnings.text: Pressing the RESET button for 5 seconds will start the de-aeration process! The boiler will run in de-aeration mode for approximately 5 minutes before restarting

source_refs.source_quote: Warning Pressing the RESET button for 5 seconds will start the de-aeration process! The boiler will run in de-aeration mode for approximately 5 minutes before restarting

safety_warnings.text: When changing components ensure that both the gas and electrical supplies to the boiler are isolated before any work is started. When the component has been changed recommission the boiler. Always examine any seals or gaskets, replacing where necessary. The case front panel MUST seal effectively against the boiler side panels.

source_refs.source_quote: Warning When changing components ensure that both the gas and electri- cal supplies to the boiler are isolated before any work is started. When the component has been changed recommission the boiler. Always examine any seals or gaskets, replacing where necessary. The case front panel MUST seal effectively against the boiler side panels.

safety_warnings.text: Before performing any operation, make sure the boiler is not powered on. Once the maintenance operations are complete, reset the original boiler operating parameters if they were changed.

source_refs.source_quote: Warning Before performing any operation, make sure the boiler is not pow- ered on. Once the maintenance operations are complete, reset the original boiler operating parameters if they were changed.

safety_warnings.text: Wait for the combustion chamber and pipes to cool down.

source_refs.source_quote: Warning Wait for the combustion chamber and pipes to cool down.

safety_warnings.text: When changing components ensure that both the gas and electrical supplies to the boiler are isolated before any work is started. When the component has been changed recommission the boiler. Always examine any seals or gaskets, replacing where necessary. The case front panel MUST seal effectively against the boiler side panels.

source_refs.source_quote: Warning When changing components ensure that both the gas and electri- cal supplies to the boiler are isolated before any work is started. When the component has been changed recommission the boiler. Always examine any seals or gaskets, replacing where necessary. The case front panel MUST seal effectively against the boiler side panels.

safety_warnings.text: Ensure all wiring and electronics are protected before draining down.

source_refs.source_quote: Warning Ensure all wiring and electronics are protected before draining down.

safety_warnings.text: Care must be taken to avoid damage due to residual water in the heat exchanger.

source_refs.source_quote: Warning Care must be taken to avoid damage due to residual water in the heat exchanger.

safety_warnings.text: Care must be taken with the heat exchanger support/spring clip when reassembling. Ensure the guides on the rear of the heat exchanger engage fully with the 2 retaining tags on the boiler chassis.

source_refs.source_quote: Warning Care must be taken with the heat exchanger support/spring clip when reassembling. Ensure the guides on the rear of the heat ex- changer engage fully with the 2 retaining tags on the boiler chas- sis.

safety_warnings.topic: Water damage when removing expansion vessel
safety_warnings.text: Take precautions to protect other components from water damage when removing the expansion vessel.

source_refs.source_quote: Warning Take precautions to protect other components from water damage when removing the expansion vessel.

safety_warnings.topic: Water damage when removing pump and auto air vent
safety_warnings.text: Take precautions to protect other components from water damage when removing the pump and auto air vent.

source_refs.source_quote: Warning Take precautions to protect other components from water damage when removing the pump and auto air vent.

safety_warnings.topic: Water damage when removing air vent
safety_warnings.text: Take precautions to protect other components from water damage when removing the air vent.

source_refs.source_quote: Warning Take precautions to protect other components from water damage when removing the air vent.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Annual Service
maintenance_tasks.description: Servicing must be performed by a competent person in accordance with BS 7967-4.

source_refs.source_quote: For reasons of safety and economy, it is recommended that the boiler is serviced annually. Servicing must be performed by a competent person in accordance with BS 7967-4.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check flue system integrity and seals
maintenance_tasks.description: Check air inlet sample to eliminate the possibility of recirculation. O2 ≥ 20.6% & CO2< 0.2%

source_refs.source_quote: During routine servicing, and after any maintenance or change of part of the combustion circuit, the following must be checked:- • The integrity of the complete flue system and the flue seals by checking air inlet sample to eliminate the possibility of recirculation. O2 ≥ 20.6% & CO2< 0.2%

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check boiler combustion circuit and relevant seals

source_refs.source_quote: • The integrity of the boiler combustion circuit and relevant seals.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check operational gas inlet pressure and gas rate

source_refs.source_quote: • The operational gas inlet pressure and the gas rate.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check combustion performance (CO/CO2 ratio)
maintenance_tasks.description: Must be less than 0.004. If greater, perform 'Standard Inspection and Maintenance' or 'Setting the Gas Valve' or 'Replace and set the gas valve'.
maintenance_tasks.required_qualification: competent person (assessed in use of flue gas analyser and interpretation of results, analyser meeting BS 7927 or BS-EN50379-3 and calibrated, CPA1 ACS assessment)

source_refs.source_quote: • The combustion performance as described in 'Check the Combustion Performance' below. Competence to carry out checking combustion performance BS 6798 'Specification for Installation & Maintenance of Gas Fired Boilers not ex- ceeding 70kWh' advises that:- • The person carrying out a combustion measurement should have been assessed as competent in the use of a flue gas analyser and the inter- pretation of the results. • The flue gas analyser used should be one meeting the requirements of BS 7927 or BS-EN50379-3 and be calibrated in accordance with the an- alyser manufacturers' requirements. • Competence can be demonstrated by satisfactory completion of the CPA1 ACS assessment, which covers the use of electronic portable combustion gas analysers in accordance with BS 7967, Parts 1 to 4. Check the Combustion Performance (CO/CO2 ratio) Set the boiler to operate at maximum rate. Remove the plug from the flue sampling point, insert the analyser probe and obtain the CO/CO2 ratio. This must be less than 0.004. If the combus- tion reading (CO/CO2 ratio) is greater than this, and the integrity of the complete flue system and combustion circuit seals has been verified, and the inlet gas pressure and gas rate are satisfactory either:- • Perform the 'Standard Inspection and Maintenance'. Perform 'Setting the Gas Valve'. • Replace and set the gas valve.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check appearance and seal of gaskets in gas and combustion circuit

source_refs.source_quote: The following checks must be performed every year in order to ensure effi- cient boiler operation: 1. Check the appearance and seal of the gaskets in the gas circuit and the combustion circuit;

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check condition and correct position of flame detection and ignition electrode

source_refs.source_quote: 2. Check the condition and correct position of the flame detection and ignition electrode;

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check condition of the burner and that it is correctly fastened

source_refs.source_quote: 3. Check the condition of the burner and that it is correctly fastened;

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check for impurities inside the combustion chamber
maintenance_tasks.description: Use a vacuum cleaner or cleaning kit.

source_refs.source_quote: 4. Check for any impurities located inside the combustion chamber. To do so, use a vacuum cleaner or the cleaning kit available as an ac- cessory;

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check pressure of the heating system
maintenance_tasks.description: Pressure must be between 1.0 and 1.5 bar. Restore if necessary.

source_refs.source_quote: 5. Check the pressure of the heating system; 10.2.1 Checking the water pressure In order for the boiler to operate correctly, the pressure of the water in the heating circuit must be between 1.0 and 1.5 bar. Restore the water pressure if necessary.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check pressure of the expansion vessel
maintenance_tasks.description: Check precharge every year and restore pressure to 1 bar if necessary. Replace if necessary.

source_refs.source_quote: 6. Check the pressure of the expansion vessel; 10.2.2 Checking the expansion vessel Check the expansion vessel and replace it if necessary. Check its precharge every year and restore the pressure to 1 bar if necessa- ry.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check that the fan is working correctly

source_refs.source_quote: 7. Check that the fan is working correctly;

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check that intake and exhaust pipes are not obstructed

source_refs.source_quote: 8. Check that the intake and exhaust pipes are not obstructed;

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check for impurities inside the siphon

source_refs.source_quote: 9. Check for any impurities inside the siphon;

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check condition of the magnesium anode (if present)
maintenance_tasks.description: For boilers equipped with a tank.

source_refs.source_quote: 10. Check the condition of the magnesium anode, if present, for boilers equipped with a tank.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check automatic air vent
maintenance_tasks.description: Check that the pump venting valve is working. Replace if leaking.

source_refs.source_quote: Check that the pump venting valve is working. In the event of a leak, replace the valve.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check detection/spark ignition electrode
maintenance_tasks.description: Check that the detection/spark electrode is not worn. Replace the electrode if necessary.

source_refs.source_quote: 7. Check that the detection/spark electrode is not worn. Replace the electrode if necessary.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check burner, gasket and insulation board
maintenance_tasks.description: Check the condition of the burner, the gasket and the insulation board.

source_refs.source_quote: 8. Check the condition of the burner, the gasket and the insulation board.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Clean heat exchanger
maintenance_tasks.description: Remove any loose deposits using a vacuum cleaner. Use a brush with plastic bristles to dislodge stubborn deposits, then vacuum. Do not use chemicals.

source_refs.source_quote: 9. Any loose deposits in the heat exchanger should be removed using a vacuum cleaner. 10. A brush with plastic bristles can be used to dislodge any stubborn deposits, which should then also be removed by vacuum. 11. Do not use any chemicals to clean the heat exchanger.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check burner surface
maintenance_tasks.description: Check that there are no cracks and/or other damage on the surface of the burner. Replace if damaged.

source_refs.source_quote: 12. The burner does not require any maintenance as it is self-cleaning. Check that there are no cracks and/or other damage on the surface of the burner. If the burner is damaged, replace it.
